title: Alkaline Lysis Plasmid Purification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4o0ig","text":"Plasmid DNA purification by alkaline lysis is a widely used molecular biology technique for isolating plasmid DNA from Escherichia coli cultures. This method leverages the unique structural properties of plasmid DNA to separate it effectively from chromosomal DNA and cellular debris, yielding high-quality plasmid suitable for downstream applications such as cloning, sequencing, PCR, and transfection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":119,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2532p","text":"Use sterile techniques to avoid contamination that may degrade plasmid DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9p7v7","text":"Adjust reagent volumes proportionally according to the culture volume (mini, midi, or maxi prep).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aub4o","text":"Mix buffers and cell suspensions gently by inversion rather than vortexing to prevent shearing of genomic DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ck90b","text":"Keep all buffers and samples cold when indicated to maintain plasmid stability and reduce nuclease activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d3a8b","text":"Always include RNase A in the resuspension buffer to remove RNA contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f42jh","text":"Perform centrifugation steps at recommended speeds and temperatures to ensure efficient separation of plasmid DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ktl6","text":"Validate plasmid DNA quality and concentration post-prep by spectrophotometry or gel electrophoresis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"18sj5","text":"Prepare all buffers fresh or ensure they are properly stored and equilibrated to appropriate temperatures before starting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6u4k3","text":"Pre-cool centrifuges, tubes, and neutralization buffer to 4°C or on ice as required.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"chno3","text":"Grow E. coli cultures overnight in LB medium with appropriate antibiotics to reach stationary phase (14–16 hours, 37°C, shaking).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":5,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3i24","text":"Confirm culture density to avoid overloading the lysis step, which can reduce purity and yield.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lf6v","text":"Have all materials including pipettes, filter tips, tubes, and reagents ready and labeled for efficient workflow.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1bduc","text":"Sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) are caustic reagents: wear gloves, protective eyewear, and lab coat when handling.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"finro","text":"Avoid vigorous vortexing or shaking after lysis buffer addition to reduce shearing of chromosomal DNA that contaminates plasmid preps.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5nahl","text":"Do not exceed recommended incubation times during lysis to prevent irreversible denaturation of plasmid DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4mss7","text":"Handle pellets gently during washing and resuspension to avoid loss of plasmid DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fpu0p","text":"Dispose of bacterial waste and chemical reagents according to institutional biosafety and chemical safety guidelines.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

centrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bi6md","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate E. coli harboring the plasmid into LB broth with appropriate antibiotic. Mini prep: 2-5 mL Midi prep: 25-100 mL Maxi prep: 250-1000 mL
Incubate Overnight at 150 rpm, 37 °C,
Centrifuge Mini prep: 12000 x g, 4 °C, 1 minute Midi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes Maxi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Discard superntatant completely.
Resuspend cell pellet in resuspension buffer: Mini prep: 250 µL Midi prep: 4 mL Maxi prep: 10 mL
Mix gently by pipetting until cells are completely resuspended.
Add lysis buffer to resuspended cells: Mini prep: 250 µL Midi prep: 4 mL Maxi prep: 10 mL
Mix gently by inverting the tube 4-6 times. Avoid vortexing to prevent shearing genomic DNA.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:04:00 . Do not exceed 5 minutes to prevent irreversible denaturation.
Add ice-cold neutralization buffer Mini prep: 350 µL Midi prep: 6 mL Maxi prep: 15 mL
Mix gently by inverting until a thick withe precipitate forms.
Incubate on ice for 00:10:00 to enhance precipitate formation.
Centrifuge Mini prep: 12000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes Midi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Maxi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes
Carefully transfer the clear supernatant to a fresh tube without disturbing the pellet.
Add ice-cold isopropanol Mini prep: 250 µL Midi prep: 4 mL Maxi prep: 10 mL
Mix by gentle inversion.
Incubate at -20 °C for 00:30:00
Centrifuge Mini prep: 12000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Midi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Maxi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes
Discard supernatant gently without disturbing pellet.
Add 1 mL of 70 % (v/v) ice-cold ethanol.
Centrifuge Mini prep: 12000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Midi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes Maxi prep: 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes
Remove ethanol carefully and air-dry pellet for 00:05:00 . Avoid over-drying.
Resuspend DNA pellet TE buffer or nuclease-free water. Mini prep: 50-100 µL Midi prep: 500-2000 µL Maxi prep: 5-20 mL
Incubate at 37 °C or Room temperature for 00:05:00 until fully dissolved.
creator: Agustín Sanchez-Temprano
